# Transcrição: 03-Definitions

- **Fonte:** 03-Definitions
- **Duração:** 02:17
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:17]

Definitions. Definitions is one of my favorite ways to make people laugh. When you're working with definitions, what you're doing is you're taking something, an object, a person, a place, anything in the world, and you're distilling it down to its very essence. So if I was to describe my cat Toby to you, I would say that he's a vomit machine, but also an amateur physicist constantly experimenting with gravity by tossing things off the table and onto the floor. So there's many things I could say about Toby. I could talk about the color of his fur, how he purrs, the way that he eats, the way he constantly tries to escape our house. But what I chose to use is a vomit machine because he vomits a lot. And he's an amateur physicist because he's always knocking things off tables. I've taken the concept of cat essentially and distilled it down to two of its very basic essences. Most cats throw up all the time and most cats toss things off surfaces most of the time. And so I've taken two of the things that makes a cat a cat and defined my cat using it. Here's another one. A lawn mower. A lawn mower is a noise producing machine that apparently only runs on Saturdays. So I've taken the concept of lawn mower, which is actually designed to cut grass. And I've distilled it down to the two things that I think are most amusing. A noise producing machine that only runs on a Saturday. So when we're using definitions, what we want to do is look at the thing that we're trying to describe and strip it away of everything except for one, two, or maybe three essential components. Take the components that make that thing what it is, but take those components that people often don't think about first. When we strip away everything else and we take things that people know, but are not the first things that they know, it'll often make people laugh. That is definitions.
